description: Error recovery catalog for Pacifica CLI agent integrations. Parse stderr output to classify errors, then apply the recovery strategy for the matching category.

auth.description: Authentication or wallet signing failure. The CLI cannot produce a valid Ed25519 signature for the configured key.
common_causes: private_key field is empty or not set in ~/.pacifica/config.json
private_key is not a valid Base58-encoded 64-byte Ed25519 secret key
account field mismatch when using an agent sub-key
Config file missing — pacifica init was never run
auth.recovery: Run `pacifica init` to reconfigure. Verify ~/.pacifica/config.json exists. If using an agent sub-key, confirm the `account` field is set to the main wallet public key.

example.suggestion: Run pacifica init to reconfigure your wallet.

rate_limit.description: The Pacifica API has returned a rate limit response (HTTP 429) or the agent is sending requests too frequently.
common_causes: Polling interval too low for copy watch or arb bot
Multiple concurrent agent sessions hitting the same endpoint
Batch operations submitting too many requests in rapid succession
rate_limit.recovery: Apply exponential backoff: wait 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s between retries. After recovery, increase poll interval. Do not retry more than 4 times before stopping the session.

example.suggestion: Back off and retry after 2 seconds.

network.description: Network connectivity failure: TCP connection refused, DNS resolution failure, or request timeout.
common_causes: Solana RPC endpoint is down or overloaded
Pacifica API server unavailable
Local internet connectivity issue
Firewall blocking outbound connections
network.recovery: Retry 3 times with 3-second linear backoff. If all retries fail, pause the session for 60 seconds before resuming. Check https://test-api.pacifica.fi/health if available.

example.suggestion: Retry in 3 seconds. Check internet connectivity.

sdk.description: Pacifica SDK or API error not caused by network or auth. Typically a malformed request, missing required field, or unexpected API response shape.
common_causes: Required request field missing (symbol, size, side)
API returned an unexpected response structure
SDK version mismatch with live API
sdk.recovery: Inspect the error message. If it describes a missing or invalid field, fix the request parameters. Do not retry without changing the request. If the API response shape changed, check for CLI updates.

example.message: Pacifica API error 400: missing required field 'symbol'

onchain.description: On-chain transaction or state error. The order was submitted but rejected by the Pacifica program or Solana runtime.

backoff.note: Only retry if the error is explicitly a timeout or RPC submission failure, not a program rejection.

common_causes: Insufficient margin for the requested position size and leverage
Position not found when attempting to close (already closed)
Reduce-only order rejected because it would increase position
Liquidation price too close to current mark price
Order size below minimum or above maximum allowed
onchain.recovery: For 'Insufficient margin': reduce position size or leverage. For 'Position not found': check `pacifica positions --json` — position may already be closed. For 'Reduce only': ensure side and amount match the existing position direction.

example.message: Trade failed: Insufficient margin for 10x leverage on $2000 position
example.suggestion: Reduce size to $1000 or lower leverage to 5x.

intelligence.description: Intelligence engine or local store error. Pattern data is missing, the store is empty, or the engine failed to process records.

backoff.note: Retry by seeding data or running the engine first, not by re-running the failed command immediately.

common_causes: Intelligence store empty — no trades have been captured yet
Pattern engine returned 0 patterns (insufficient trade records)
Intelligence API server not running when making REST calls
Local store file corrupted or missing permissions
intelligence.recovery: If store is empty: place some trades to capture intelligence records, or run `pacifica intelligence seed --count 100` in development. Then run `pacifica intelligence run` to compute patterns. For API server: start with `pacifica intelligence serve`.

example.message: No patterns detected. Run 'intelligence seed' to add data first.
example.suggestion: Run pacifica intelligence seed --count 100, then pacifica intelligence run.

guardrail.description: The agent guardrail system blocked a trade or action. This is a deliberate safety rejection, not a system error.
common_causes: Daily spending limit exhausted for the current UTC day
Single order size exceeds max_order_size config
Leverage requested exceeds max_leverage config
Action type is in the blocked_actions list
agent.enabled is false (kill switch activated)
guardrail.recovery: Check `pacifica agent status --json`. If the daily limit is exhausted, wait until UTC midnight for reset. If a single-order limit is too low, use `pacifica agent config` to adjust. If the agent is disabled, run `pacifica agent start` to re-enable (requires human confirmation). Never override guardrails autonomously.

example.message: Rejected: daily spending limit of $2000 would be exceeded ($1900 spent, $200 order)
example.suggestion: Wait until UTC midnight or adjust the daily limit with `pacifica agent config`.

config.description: Configuration file is missing, unreadable, or contains invalid JSON.
common_causes: ~/.pacifica/config.json does not exist — pacifica init was never run
Config file has invalid JSON syntax
File permissions prevent reading (chmod issue)
Required field is missing from config
config.recovery: Run `pacifica init` to create or repair the configuration file. Check that ~/.pacifica/ directory exists and is readable.

example.message: Config not found at ~/.pacifica/config.json. Run pacifica init.
example.suggestion: Run pacifica init to create the configuration.

parse.description: A CLI argument or flag value failed to parse. The command was not executed.
common_causes: Non-numeric value passed to a numeric flag (e.g. --leverage abc)
Invalid order type (not market or limit)
Side argument is not 'long' or 'short' for simulate
Invalid date format for journal export --from/--to flags
parse.recovery: Fix the argument value and re-run. Refer to the command's --help output or the tool catalog for valid parameter types and ranges.

validation.description: Business logic validation failure. Inputs were syntactically valid but failed semantic validation.
common_causes: Symbol not found in the list of active Pacifica markets
Position size is zero or negative
Limit order submitted without a --price flag
Alert ID prefix does not match any stored alert
Arb position ID not found
validation.recovery: Run `pacifica scan --json` to get the list of valid symbols. Verify the order ID with `pacifica orders --json`. Check alert IDs with `pacifica alerts list --json`.

example.message: Market "DOGE-USDC-PERP" not found. Try: DOGE or DOGE-USDC-PERP
example.suggestion: Run pacifica scan --json to see available markets.
